steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Rhetorical Synthesis question. The goal is to emphasize Janaki Ammal’s achievement — specifically, what she personally accomplished and how. The correct answer must make Ammal the active subject and name both her method (crossbreeding) and her result (climate-suited hybrids).

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘By crossbreeding the imported sugarcane species Saccharum officinarum with grasses native to India, Ammal succeeded in creating sugarcane hybrids well suited to India's climate.’: Ammal is the active grammatical subject. The sentence names her method (crossbreeding Saccharum officinarum with native grasses) and her specific achievement (climate-suited hybrids) — exactly what the goal requires. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘In the 1930s, the Imperial Sugar Cane Institute, which enlisted Ammal, sought to limit dependence on imported sugarcane.’: The subject and emphasis belong to the Institute, not Ammal. Her achievement is not mentioned. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Ammal was enlisted by the Imperial Sugar Cane Institute at a time when a local variety of sugarcane needed to be produced.’: Ammal appears in passive voice as someone being enlisted, and her actual achievement — successfully creating hybrids — is absent entirely. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘As part of efforts to breed a local variety of sugarcane, an imported sugarcane species called Saccharum officinarum was crossbred with grasses native to India.’: Written in passive voice with no named subject — Ammal’s name does not appear, so the achievement is not attributed to her. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
